title: Rich Smoked Jamaican Pork Skillet
description: A rich and flavorful skillet dish featuring caramelized pork, smoked in a charcoal vessel and served with a tangy sauce, perfect for a date night at home.
instructions: Preheat the binchotan charcoal in a charcoal chimney until coals are ashy and smoky, then transfer to a tava-griddle or large cast-iron skillet.
Season the pork shoulder with salt, black pepper, smoked paprika, garlic powder, and cumin.
Sear the pork shoulder in the smoky charcoal vessel for 5 minutes on each side, or until browned and crispy.
Transfer the pork shoulder to a plate and set aside, then add the sliced ginger to the vessel and cook for 2 minutes, or until fragrant.
Add the apple cider vinegar to the vessel and bring to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Return the pork shoulder to the vessel and spoon some of the simmering sauce over the top, then cover with a lid and transfer to the oven at 30020F (15020C) for 2 1/2 hours, or until tender.
While the pork is cooking, caramelize the brown sugar in a small saucepan over medium heat, stirring frequently, until dark and sticky.
Once the pork is done, transfer it back to the tava-griddle and spoon some of the caramelized brown sugar on top.
Drizzle with caramel sauce and sprinkle with chopped cilantro and serve hot.
Let the pork rest for 10 minutes before slicing into thick chops and serving.
If desired, serve with additional caramel sauce on the side for drizzling.
